```{r}

##################
## Package loading
##################

# Install MrBeanApp from GitHub
remotes::install_github("AparicioJohan/MrBeanApp")

# Load required libraries and MrBean
library(MrBean)
library(asreml)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(corrplot)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Run the MrBean app and activate ASReml license
MrBean::run_app()
asreml.license.activate()

```

```{r}

####################################################################################################################
## Use MrBean interface to extract BLUEs for each standardized trait within each environment
# - Used AR1 x AR1 row x column corrections in ASReml for all envs. except for 22THD (used SpATS)
  # - Run genotype as fixed to obtain estimates (BLUEs) for each genotype in each environment, and precision weights
# - Obtain within-environment repeatabilities by running genotype as random in SpATS 
####################################################################################################################

```

```{r}

##################################################################################
## Load dataset containing all BLUEs in all environments and define traits/weights
##################################################################################

# Load the dataset from the specified path
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Mr. Bean BLUEs/BLUEs_standardized data.csv", stringsAsFactors = TRUE)

# Ensure Environment and Genotype columns are set as factors
data$Environment <- as.factor(data$Environment)
data$Genotype <- as.factor(data$Genotype)

# Convert columns from the 3rd to the last to numeric
data[, 4:ncol(data)] <- lapply(data[, 4:ncol(data)], function(x) {
  if (is.factor(x)) {
    as.numeric(as.character(x)) # Convert Factor to Character, then to Numeric
  } else if (is.character(x)) {
    as.numeric(x) # Directly convert Character to Numeric
  } else {
    x # Keep as is if already numeric
  }
})

# View the structure again to confirm changes
str(data)

# Define the traits and corresponding weights
traits <- c(
  "HD_blue", "SD_blue", "GFP_blue", "PH_blue", "TC_blue", "NDVI_blue", "GR_blue", 
  "GY_blue", "TKW_blue", "TW_blue", "KA_blue", "KL_blue", "KW_blue", "GP_blue", 
  "GPD_blue", "PQS_blue", "SRC_blue", 
  "B_blue", "Na_blue", "Mg_blue", "Al_blue", "P_blue", "K_blue", "Ca_blue", 
  "Fe_blue", "Mn_blue", "Co_blue", "Ni_blue", "Cu_blue", "Zn_blue", "As_blue", 
  "Se_blue", "Rb_blue", "Sr_blue", "Mo_blue", "Cd_blue", 
  "NDVI_till_blue", "NDVI_joint_blue", "NDVI_boot_blue", "NDVI_anth_blue", 
  "NDVI_milk_blue", 
  "GR_till_blue", "GR_joint_blue", "GR_boot_blue", "GR_anth_blue", "GR_milk_blue"
)

# Define weights for each trait in the same order
weights <- c(
  "HD_weight", "SD_weight", "GFP_weight", "PH_weight", "TC_weight", "NDVI_weight", 
  "GR_weight", "GY_weight", "TKW_weight", "TW_weight", "KA_weight", "KL_weight", 
  "KW_weight", "GP_weight", "GPD_weight", "PQS_weight", "SRC_weight", 
  "B_weight", "Na_weight", "Mg_weight", "Al_weight", "P_weight", "K_weight", 
  "Ca_weight", "Fe_weight", "Mn_weight", "Co_weight", "Ni_weight", "Cu_weight", 
  "Zn_weight", "As_weight", "Se_weight", "Rb_weight", "Sr_weight", "Mo_weight", 
  "Cd_weight", 
  "NDVI_till_weight", "NDVI_joint_weight", "NDVI_boot_weight", "NDVI_anth_weight", 
  "NDVI_milk_weight", 
  "GR_till_weight", "GR_joint_weight", "GR_boot_weight", "GR_anth_weight", 
  "GR_milk_weight"
)

```

```{r}

##########################################################################
## Run ASReml mixed model analysis to obtain BLUPs using BLUEs and weights
# - Output files created:
  # - Genetic repeatabilities for each trait
  # - BLUPs for each trait
  # - Variance components for each trait
  # - Fixed effect estimates for each trait within each environment
##########################################################################

# Initialize lists
heritabilities <- list()
blups_list <- list()
varcomps <- list()
fixef_list <- list()

# Iterate over each trait and its corresponding weight
for (i in seq_along(traits)) {
  trait <- traits[i]
  weight <- weights[i]
  
  # Filter the dataset to keep rows with non-missing values for the current trait and weight
  trait_data <- data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait]]) & !is.na(.data[[weight]])) %>%
    select(Environment, Genotype, trait_value = all_of(trait), weight_value = all_of(weight))
  
  # Fit a linear mixed model using ASReml for the current trait
  # Fixed effect: Environment
  # Random effects: Genotype (main effect) and Genotype x Environment (GxE interaction)
  # Weight observations by the specified weight for the current trait
  # Start values constraint: set residual variance ('units!R') to 1 and fix it during fitting
  asreml_model <- asreml(
    fixed = trait_value ~ 0 + Environment, # Fixed effect for environment
    random = ~ Genotype + Environment:Genotype, # Random effects for Genotype and GxE
    rcov = ~ idv(units),
    weights = weight_value, # Use corresponding weights
    data = trait_data,
    na.action = na.method("include"),
    family = asr_gaussian(dispersion = 1)
  )

  # Check convergence and extract model summary
  model_summary <- summary(asreml_model, coef = TRUE)
  vc <- model_summary$varcomp  # Extract variance components
  fe <- asreml_model$coefficients$fixed
  
  varcomps[[trait]] <- vc
  fixef_list[[trait]] <- fe

  # Extract the genetic variance and GxE variance from the variance components
  genetic_var <- vc["Genotype", "component"]
  gxe_var <- vc["Environment:Genotype", "component"]

  # Calculate repeatability as the proportion of genetic variance to total variance (genetic + GxE)
  heritability <- genetic_var / (genetic_var + gxe_var)
  heritabilities[[trait]] <- heritability  # Store repeatability in list for current trait

  # Predict BLUPs for each genotype and store them in the list
  blups <- predict(asreml_model, classify = "Genotype")$pvals
  blups_list[[trait]] <- blups

  # Print results for the current trait
  cat("Trait:", trait, "\n")
  cat("Genetic Variance:", genetic_var, "\n")
  cat("GxE Variance:", gxe_var, "\n")
  cat("Heritability:", heritability, "\n")
  cat("BLUPs for", trait, "(first few):\n")
  print(head(blups))  # Display the first few BLUP values
  cat("\n")
}

# Convert the list to a data frame
heritabilities_df <- data.frame(
  Trait = names(heritabilities),
  Heritability = unlist(heritabilities)
)

# Save the data frame to a CSV file
write.csv(heritabilities_df, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs/heritabilities.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Reuse the same output folder you used for heritabilities
out_dir <- "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Variance components across traits
vc_long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(varcomps), function(tr) {
  vc <- varcomps[[tr]]
  if (!is.data.frame(vc)) vc <- as.data.frame(vc)
  if (is.null(vc$Term)) {
    vc$Term <- rownames(vc)
    rownames(vc) <- NULL
  }
  vc$Trait <- tr
  cols <- c("Trait","Term", setdiff(names(vc), c("Trait","Term")))
  vc[, cols, drop = FALSE]
}))
vc_long <- vc_long[order(vc_long$Trait, vc_long$Term), ]

write.csv(vc_long,
          file.path(out_dir, "variance_components_all_traits.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# Fixed effects across traits
fixef_long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(fixef_list), function(tr) {
  fe <- fixef_list[[tr]]
  if (is.null(fe)) return(NULL)

  if (is.atomic(fe) && is.null(dim(fe))) {
    df <- data.frame(Term = names(fe), Estimate = as.numeric(fe), SE = NA_real_)
  } else {
    df <- as.data.frame(fe)
    if (!"Term" %in% names(df)) {
      df$Term <- rownames(df)
      rownames(df) <- NULL
    }
    names(df) <- sub("(?i)^estimate$", "Estimate", names(df), perl = TRUE)
    names(df) <- sub("(?i)^std\\.error$", "SE", names(df), perl = TRUE)
  }
  df$Trait <- tr
  cols <- c("Trait","Term",
            intersect(c("Estimate","SE","z.ratio","wald","p.value"), names(df)),
            setdiff(names(df), c("Trait","Term","Estimate","SE","z.ratio","wald","p.value")))
  df[, unique(cols), drop = FALSE]
}))
if (!is.null(fixef_long) && nrow(fixef_long)) {
  fixef_long <- fixef_long[order(fixef_long$Trait, fixef_long$Term), ]
  write.csv(fixef_long,
            file.path(out_dir, "fixed_effects_all_traits.csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
}

# Genotype BLUPs across traits
blup_long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(blups_list), function(tr) {
  pv <- blups_list[[tr]]
  if (is.null(pv)) return(NULL)
  df <- as.data.frame(pv)
  nml <- tolower(names(df))
  geno_col <- names(df)[match("genotype", nml)]
  pred_col <- names(df)[match("predicted.value", nml)]
  se_col   <- names(df)[match("std.error", nml)]
  if (is.na(geno_col)) geno_col <- names(df)[grep("genotype", nml, fixed = TRUE)][1]
  if (is.na(pred_col)) pred_col <- names(df)[grep("pred", nml)][1]

  out <- data.frame(
    Trait    = tr,
    Genotype = df[[geno_col]],
    Pred     = df[[pred_col]],
    SE       = if (!is.na(se_col)) df[[se_col]] else NA_real_
  )
  out
}))
if (!is.null(blup_long) && nrow(blup_long)) {
  blup_long <- blup_long[order(blup_long$Trait, blup_long$Genotype), ]
  write.csv(blup_long,
            file.path(out_dir, "genotype_blups_all_traits.csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
}

# Rename and combine BLUPs for all traits
blups_renamed <- purrr::map2(blups_list, names(blups_list), ~ .x %>%
                                select(Genotype, predicted.value) %>%
                                rename(!!str_remove(.y, "_blue") := predicted.value))

# Combine all BLUPs into a single data frame
blups_df <- purrr::reduce(blups_renamed, full_join, by = "Genotype")

# Save the combined BLUPs to a CSV file
write.csv(blups_df, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs/standardized data_BLUPs.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Confirm "_blue" was removed from column names
print(head(blups_df))

```

```{r}

#####################################################################################
## Plot trait distributions with one facet for each environment (BLUEs) and for BLUPs
#####################################################################################

# Plotting configuration
cfg <- list(
  rp_name         = "KS061278M-4",
  checks          = c("Billings","Joe","Showdown","SY Monument","WB 4699"),
  check_colors    = c(
    "Billings"    = "#1b9e77",
    "Joe"         = "#7570b3",
    "Showdown"    = "#e7298a",
    "SY Monument" = "#66a61e",
    "WB 4699"     = "#e6ab02"
  ),
  binwidth        = 0.25,
  boundary        = 0,
  blup_label      = "Across-env (BLUP)",
  env_order       = c("2021 THD","2022 EL","2022 RL","2022 THD","2023 RL","2023 THD"),
  place_blup_top  = TRUE,
  label_rp        = TRUE,

  # spacing / stacking
  use_relative     = TRUE,
  label_pad_rel    = 0,
  label_step_rel   = 0.3,
  x_stack_tol_rel  = 0.005,
  x_stack_tol      = 0,  

  # rendering
  rp_color         = "#d95f02",
  rp_size          = 1.5,
  non_rp_size      = 0.6,

  # export
  out_dir          = "histograms_by_trait_png",
  width_in         = 12,
  height_in        = 8,
  dpi              = 300
)

`%||%` <- function(a,b) if (is.null(a)) b else a

# Plotting function
plot_blue_blup_hists <- function(trait, cfg) {

  stopifnot(all(c("Environment","Genotype","Group") %in% names(data)))
  stopifnot(all(c("Genotype", trait) %in% names(blups_df)))

  col_blue <- paste0(trait, "_blue")
  stopifnot(col_blue %in% names(data))

  d2 <- data
  d2$Environment <- as.character(d2$Environment)
  d2$Genotype    <- trimws(as.character(d2$Genotype))

  bl <- blups_df
  bl$Genotype <- trimws(as.character(bl$Genotype))

  # WILs (within-env BLUEs)
  wil_blue <- dplyr::filter(d2, Group == "WIL", !is.na(.data[[col_blue]])) |>
    dplyr::transmute(Environment, Genotype, value = .data[[col_blue]])

  # Anchors (RP + checks) within-env
  anchors_blue <- dplyr::filter(d2, Group != "WIL", !is.na(.data[[col_blue]])) |>
    dplyr::transmute(Environment,
                     Genotype,
                     Legend = dplyr::if_else(Genotype == cfg$rp_name, "RP", Genotype),
                     value = .data[[col_blue]])

  # Group map
  gmap <- dplyr::distinct(d2, Genotype, Group)

  # WILs (across-env BLUP)
  wil_blup <- bl |>
    dplyr::select(Genotype, value = dplyr::all_of(trait)) |>
    dplyr::left_join(gmap, by = "Genotype") |>
    dplyr::filter(Group == "WIL") |>
    dplyr::transmute(Environment = cfg$blup_label, Genotype, value)

  # Anchors (across-env BLUP)
  anchors_blup <- bl |>
    dplyr::select(Genotype, value = dplyr::all_of(trait)) |>
    dplyr::left_join(gmap, by = "Genotype") |>
    dplyr::filter(Group != "WIL") |>
    dplyr::transmute(Environment = cfg$blup_label,
                     Genotype,
                     Legend = dplyr::if_else(Genotype == cfg$rp_name, "RP", Genotype),
                     value)

  wil_all     <- dplyr::bind_rows(wil_blue, wil_blup)
  anchors_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(anchors_blue, anchors_blup)

  env_order <- cfg$env_order %||% unique(d2$Environment)
  env_levels <- if (cfg$place_blup_top) c(cfg$blup_label, env_order) else c(env_order, cfg$blup_label)
  wil_all$Environment     <- factor(wil_all$Environment,     levels = env_levels)
  anchors_all$Environment <- factor(anchors_all$Environment, levels = env_levels)

  # Bin id
  bin_id <- function(x) cfg$boundary + cfg$binwidth * floor((x - cfg$boundary) / cfg$binwidth)
  wil_all$bin_lo     <- bin_id(wil_all$value)
  anchors_all$bin_lo <- bin_id(anchors_all$value)

  # Bar heights and per-panel max
  bar_counts <- wil_all |>
    dplyr::count(Environment, bin_lo, name = "bar_n")

  env_max <- bar_counts |>
    dplyr::group_by(Environment) |>
    dplyr::summarise(max_n = dplyr::if_else(any(bar_n > 0), max(bar_n), 0L), .groups = "drop")

  # Codes for checks
  checks <- trimws(cfg$checks)
  c_map  <- stats::setNames(paste0("C", seq_along(checks)), checks)

  base_df <- anchors_all |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      short = dplyr::case_when(
        Legend == "RP" &  cfg$label_rp ~ "RP",
        Legend == "RP" & !cfg$label_rp ~ NA_character_,
        Legend %in% names(c_map)       ~ c_map[Legend],
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(bar_counts, by = c("Environment","bin_lo")) |>
    dplyr::mutate(bar_n = dplyr::coalesce(bar_n, 0L)) |>
    dplyr::left_join(env_max, by = "Environment")

  x_all  <- range(c(wil_all$value, anchors_all$value), na.rm = TRUE)
  x_span <- diff(x_all)
  tol_global <- max(
    cfg$x_stack_tol,
    cfg$x_stack_tol_rel * x_span,
    cfg$binwidth * 0.80
  )

  env_tol <- data.frame(Environment = levels(wil_all$Environment), tol_env = tol_global)
  base_df <- base_df |> dplyr::left_join(env_tol, by = "Environment")

  vis_stack <- base_df |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(short)) |>
    dplyr::group_by(Environment) |>
    dplyr::arrange(value, .by_group = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      gap     = value - dplyr::lag(value),
      samebin = bin_lo == dplyr::lag(bin_lo),
      new_cluster = dplyr::if_else(is.na(gap) | (!samebin & gap > tol_env), 1L, 0L),
      cluster_id  = cumsum(new_cluster)
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup() |>
    dplyr::group_by(Environment, cluster_id) |>
    dplyr::arrange(value, .by_group = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(stack_i = dplyr::row_number()) |>
    dplyr::ungroup() |>
    dplyr::select(Environment, Genotype, bin_lo, value, short, bar_n, max_n, cluster_id, stack_i)

  base_df <- base_df |>
    dplyr::left_join(vis_stack[, c("Environment","Genotype","cluster_id","stack_i")],
                     by = c("Environment","Genotype"))

  pad_step_df <- base_df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      pad_ct  = if (cfg$use_relative) (cfg$label_pad_rel  * pmax(1, max_n)) else cfg$label_pad,
      step_ct = if (cfg$use_relative) (cfg$label_step_rel * pmax(1, max_n)) else cfg$label_step
    )

  lab_df <- pad_step_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(Environment, cluster_id) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      base_y = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(cluster_id), max(bar_n, na.rm = TRUE), bar_n)
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup() |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      y_lab = dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(short),
        base_y + pad_ct + (dplyr::coalesce(stack_i, 1L) - 1L) * step_ct,
        NA_real_
      )
    )

  seg_df <- lab_df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      seg_top = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(y_lab),
                               pmax(0, y_lab - 0.2 * step_ct),
                               bar_n + 0.1 * pad_ct)
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(Environment, bin_lo, Genotype, seg_top)

  anchors_plot <- anchors_all |>
    dplyr::left_join(seg_df, by = c("Environment","bin_lo","Genotype"))

  legend_levels <- unique(c("RP", checks))
  present       <- sort(unique(anchors_all$Legend))
  legend_levels <- legend_levels[legend_levels %in% present]

  color_map <- stats::setNames(rep("#BBBBBB", length(legend_levels)), legend_levels)
  size_map  <- stats::setNames(rep(cfg$non_rp_size, length(legend_levels)), legend_levels)
  color_map[names(cfg$check_colors)] <- cfg$check_colors
  if ("RP" %in% legend_levels) { color_map["RP"] <- cfg$rp_color; size_map["RP"] <- cfg$rp_size }

  pad    <- max(0.1, x_span * 0.03)
  x_lim  <- c(x_all[1] - pad, x_all[2] + pad)

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(wil_all, ggplot2::aes(x = value)) +
    ggplot2::geom_histogram(
      binwidth = cfg$binwidth, boundary = cfg$boundary,
      fill = "#F7F7F7", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_segment(
      data = dplyr::filter(anchors_plot, Legend != "RP"),
      ggplot2::aes(x = value, xend = value, y = 0, yend = seg_top,
                   color = Legend, linewidth = Legend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_segment(
      data = dplyr::filter(anchors_plot, Legend == "RP"),
      ggplot2::aes(x = value, xend = value, y = 0, yend = seg_top,
                   color = Legend, linewidth = Legend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_label(
      data = dplyr::filter(lab_df, !is.na(short)),
      ggplot2::aes(x = value, y = y_lab, label = short, color = Legend),
      fill = "white", label.size = 0, label.r = grid::unit(0, "pt"),
      vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = color_map, limits = names(color_map), drop = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::scale_linewidth_manual(values = size_map, limits = names(size_map), drop = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~Environment, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste(trait, "histograms"),
      x = "Standardized value (SD units)",
      y = "Number of WILs",
      color = NULL, linewidth = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 12, r = 16, b = 8, l = 10)
    ) +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = x_lim, clip = "off")

  print(p); invisible(p)
}

# Batch across traits and export plots
all_traits <- setdiff(names(blups_df), "Genotype")
traits <- all_traits[vapply(all_traits, function(tr) paste0(tr, "_blue") %in% names(data), logical(1))]

if (!dir.exists(cfg$out_dir)) dir.create(cfg$out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (tr in traits) {
  message("→ Making plot for trait: ", tr)
  p <- try(plot_blue_blup_hists(tr, cfg), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(p, "try-error")) { message("   ❌ Skipping ", tr, " due to error."); next }

  fname <- file.path(cfg$out_dir, sprintf("%s_hist_bin%s.png", tr, format(cfg$binwidth, trim = TRUE)))
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename = fname, plot = p,
                  width = cfg$width_in, height = cfg$height_in, dpi = cfg$dpi, units = "in")
  message("   ✅ Saved: ", fname)
}

```

```{r}

####################
## BLUP correlations
####################

# Convert BLUPs data (excluding Genotype column) to a matrix
blups_matrix <- blups_df %>% select(-Genotype) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute Pearson correlation matrix (phenotypic correlations)
phenotypic_cor_matrix <- cor(blups_matrix, method = "pearson")

write.csv(phenotypic_cor_matrix, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs/BLUP_pearson_correlation.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs/BLUP_pearson_correlation.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot.
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/ASReml-R BLUPs/BLUP_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1600, height = 1550) 

# Sets margins around the plot - right margin leaves room for the legend
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme and number of gradient levels
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text
# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()

```

```{r}

####################
## BLUE correlations
####################

## 2021 THD

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/21THD.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/21THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/21THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/21THD_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1600, height = 1600) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()


## 2022 EL

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22EL.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22EL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22EL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22EL_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1000, height = 1000) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()


## 2022 RL

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22RL.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22RL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22RL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22RL_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1000, height = 1000) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()


## 2022 THD

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22THD.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/22THD_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1150, height = 1150) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()


## 2023 RL

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23RL.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23RL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23RL_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23RL_corrplot.png", 
    width = 700, height = 650) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()


## 2023 THD

# Read the CSV file
blues <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23THD.csv", row.names = 1)

# Convert all columns to numeric
blues <- as.data.frame(lapply(blues, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))

# Check the structure of the data
str(blues)

# Calculate the Pearson correlation matrix
pearson_correlation <- cor(blues, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Save the Pearson correlation matrix to a CSV file
write.csv(pearson_correlation, file = "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Reads the saved Pearson correlation matrix from the CSV file and converts it to a matrix
pheno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23THD_pheno_corr.csv", row.names = 1)
pheno_matrix <- as.matrix(pheno)  

# Sets up the PNG file to save the plot
png("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Correlations/Between traits within environment/23THD_corrplot.png", 
    width = 1150, height = 1150) 

# Sets margins around the plot
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 1)) 

# Set color scheme
color_scheme <- COL2('PuOr', 20)

# Plot the Pearson correlation matrix in the lower triangle
corrplot(pheno_matrix, 
         method = "color", 
         type = "lower",  # Plot only the lower triangle
         addCoef.col = 'black',  # Displays correlation coefficients in black
         number.cex = 1,  # Adjusts coefficient text size
         tl.srt = 45,  # Rotates text labels for better readability
         tl.col = "black",  # Sets text label color
         tl.cex = 1,  # Adjusts text label size for readability
         tl.pos = "ld",  # Positions labels on the left and top
         col = color_scheme,  # Applies the color scheme
         cl.pos = "b",  # Positions the color legend on the right
         cl.ratio = 0.1,  # Narrows the width of the legend
         cl.cex = 0.8)  # Adjusts the size of the legend text

# Finalizes the plot and saves it as a PNG file
dev.off()

```
